_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Portal decompression is indicated in patients who have portal hypertension complicated by gastrointestinal hemorrhage from esophageal varices that are not effectively controlled with sclerotherapy injections.

Mesenteric caval shunt is necessary in patients who have undergone splenectomy and have either thrombosis or cavernomatous changes of the portal vein. The mesocaval shunt is advisable in patients with excessive bleeding at surgery from periportal or perisplenic vessels.
Splenic vein is not dilated or thrombosed.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication of procedure and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Was clinical evaluation and imaging indicative of surgery?
Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did Clinical evaluation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical evaluation and imaging not indicative of surgery.
Clinical notes lacking detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure at pre-authorization.
Clinical evaluation and imaging did not confirm the diagnosis at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) missing daily vitals or line of treatment at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not available at claim submission.
Imaging not indicative of surgery at claim submission.
Discharge summary missing follow-up advice at the time of discharge for claim submission.
Missing Complete blood count at pre-authorization.
Missing Liver function tests at pre-authorization.
Missing USG/CT Abdomen report at pre-authorization.

clinical_key_pointers: Portal hypertension is defined as elevated pressure in portal venous system due to resistance to portal blood flow. Pressure more than 10 mmHg is defined as portal hypertension. Portal hypertension is most commonly caused by cirrhosis, but can also be seen in portal vein obstruction from unknown causes.
Consequences of portal hypertension: Esophageal varices
Consequences of portal hypertension: Caput Medusae
surgical_options: Devascularization procedure: Gastric transection of Tanner
Devascularization procedure: Sugiura and Futugawa operation
Portosystemic shunt procedures: Side-to-side portacaval shunt
Portosystemic shunt procedures: End-to-side portacaval shunt
Splenorenal shunt: Proximal splenorenal shunt (spleen is removed)
